# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Java Spring Boot application. Copy the JAR file, expose port 8080, and run the application. This needs to deploy quickly for our CI/CD pipeline. We'll add health checks and monitoring in phase 2.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Java runtime as a parent image
FROM openjdk:16-alpine3
ARG JAR_FILE=target/*.jar

# Set the working directory inside the container to /app, and copy both your jar file into that folder from localhost at run time (as we want our app volume mounted here) 
WORKDIR /app
COPY ${JAR_FILE} .    # this line copies local jars/libs over on top of base image.
ENTRYPOINT ["java","-Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom", "-jar", "springbootjardockerizeditorialhealthcheckmonitoringinphase2additionallayoutaskscontainerbasedonapriorivertaskdependency1andtomorrowtaskschedulingprincipalsecondsbetweentakeservicerestart"]
EXPOSE 8080   # To allow connections from outside world for port used by application to be exposed.    
CMD ["catalina.sh", "run"]    # this runs the Java jar file as a CATALINA server (default command in Docker image) with JVM arguments specified
